response.justification: Bogdan has solid full-stack engineering fundamentals and genuine high-scale distributed systems exposure from his time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, but the core competencies this role demands — experimentation frameworks, A/B testing infrastructure, telemetry and metrics platform design, rollout risk management, and growth measurement systems — are entirely absent from his resume. His career trajectory sits at Senior/Staff IC, not Principal IC6, and there is no evidence of cross-team architectural leadership or multi-workstream initiative ownership at the scale the job description requires. The mismatch is domain-deep, not just a title gap.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience: built low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Broad language portfolio covering JavaScript/TypeScript, C/C++, Java, C#, and DLang, satisfying the role's language-proficiency requirements
Demonstrated testing and quality culture: authored open-source TDD tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), ran public workshops, and applied TDD/CI/CD practices across multiple employers
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience: no mention of A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, experiment assignment, bias detection, or rollout automation anywhere in the resume
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background: the role requires owning instrumentation foundations and experimental analysis pipelines, which are entirely unrepresented

Seniority and scope gap: current and past roles are Senior/Staff IC with no evidence of Principal-level cross-team architectural ownership, unified-measurement initiatives, or multi-workstream technical leadership

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or growth measurement experience
